data.title: OpenAI makes low-cost ChatGPT Go plan available worldwide; starts ad testing
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) said on Friday that its low-cost ChatGPT Go subscription plan is now available around the world, as the generative artificial intelligence company looks to boost growth. The company also confirmed its plan to introduce advertising into ChatGPT, further placing in competition with other tech giants, such as Google (GOOG) (GOGOL).

“In markets where Go has been available, we’ve seen strong adoption and regular everyday use for tasks like writing, learning, image creation, and problem-solving,” OpenAI said on its website. “This early momentum helped inform our decision to make ChatGPT Go available globally.” The plan, initially launched in August 2025, is now available in more than 170 countries and is OpenAI's “fastest growing plan and among the most affordable AI subscription globally.”

Included in ChatGPT is access to OpenAI's latest model, GPT-5.2 Instant, as well as 10 times more messages, uploads and image creation, when compared to the free version of ChatGPT. It also offers users longer memory and context window, OpenAI added.

ChatGPT Go is priced at $8 per month in the U.S., compared to ChatGPT Plus and ChatGPT Pro, which are priced at $20 and $200 per month, respectively.

Ads push

In addition, OpenAI said on Friday that it would start testing ads in the free tier and ChatGPT Go in the U.S. “soon.” Its ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business and Enterprise plans will remain ad-free, the Sam Altman-led company added.

“The best ads are useful, entertaining, and help people discover new products and services,” OpenAI added. “Given what AI can do, we're excited to develop new experiences over time that people find more helpful and relevant than any other ads. Conversational interfaces create possibilities for people to go beyond static messages and links. For example, soon you might see an ad and be able to directly ask the questions you need to make a purchase decision.”

Last month, it was reported that OpenAI was making progress with its push to show advertising inside its near-ubiquitous chatbot.

OpenAI’s CEO of Applications Fidji Simo has been looking for someone to oversee monetization efforts, including bringing advertising to ChatGPT. It was reported in September that Simo had met with potential candidates, including some of her former colleagues from Facebook (META) to lead a new team that will bring ads to ChatGPT.

The push into advertising could further boost OpenAI's revenue, especially important as the company looks set to raise another significant funding round, perhaps gathering another $100B from investors. In November, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said the ChatGPT maker was doing “well more” than $13B in revenue and suggested it could hit $100B in revenue in 2027. OpenAI currently generates revenue from selling subscriptions to ChatGPT to consumers and businesses alike, as well as providing access to its application programming interface and other tools.

data.title: Netflix streaming turns 19: Remembering the day the iconic red DVD envelope became extinct
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) started streaming 19 years ago today when it launched its “Watch Now” feature with around 1,000 titles on January 16, 2007. At its inception, Netflix (NFLX) streaming had video quality that was lower than DVDs, and the standard $5.99-a-month disc plan initially bought subscribers just six hours of streaming per month.

 

 

Of course, the company made a revolutionary transformation by offering unlimited streaming at no additional cost to rental-disc subscribers in 2008, and by 2010 CEO Reed Hastings confirmed the company had evolved from its DVD-by-mail roots. Within another two years, Netflix (NFLX) was producing original content such as House of Cards and Lilyhammer, setting the stage for other streaming players like Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), HBO (WBD), Hulu (DIS), and Disney+ (DIS). Netflix (NFLX) is now estimated to have more than 315M total global subscribers, although the company no longer reports official tallies.

On January 16, 2007, Netflix's (NFLX) stock closed at $0.32 per share on a split-adjusted basis. On Friday afternoon, the streaming stock was swapping hands at $88.09 per share. 

Looking to the future, Netflix (NFLX) is due to report fourth-quarter earnings next week in what will be closely watched reports in the media sector.

Netflix is expected to post fourth-quarter EPS of $0.55 on revenue of $11.97 billion.

“With much still to prove, we think Netflix is positioning for substantial growth in global advertising, and that should not be overlooked,” said Wedbush analyst Alicia Reese, adding that the firm expects ad revenue to “become Netflix’s primary revenue driver in 2026, with significant opportunities in 2027.”

Seeking Alpha analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings consider the stock a Hold, while Wall Street analysts are bullish and have it slotted at Buy.

data.title: Genmab down after trial data for AbbVie-partnered Epkinly in B-cell lymphoma
data.description:
Genmab (GMAB) on Friday announced that its bispecific antibody epcoritamab, marketed as Epkinly in partnership with AbbVie (ABBV), extended patient survival without tumor progression in a late-stage trial for diffuse large B-cell lymphoma ((DLBCL)).

However, Genmab (GMAB) lost ~7% after the readout, while AbbVie (ABB) traded flat.

Epcoritamab is currently available in the U.S. under the FDA's accelerated approval pathway for DLBCL patients whose cancer has come back (relapsed) or failed to respond to two or more treatments (refractory).

Citing topline data from a Phase 3 trial called EPCORE DLBCL-1, the Danish biotech said that epcoritamab as a single agent also improved patients’ complete response rates, duration of response, and time to next therapy.

However, there was no statistically significant improvement for overall survival, the gold standard used to measure the efficacy of a cancer therapy. The 483-patient global trial enrolled R/R DLBCL patients who had received at least one prior line of therapy.

The injectable indicated a tolerability profile consistent with its known safety findings, Genmab (GMAB) said, adding that it will meet with global regulators to discuss the next steps for the program.

data.title: TTWO trades in red for seventh straight session
data.description:
Shares of Take-Two Interactive (TTWO) fell 1.85% to $239.81 in afternoon trade on Friday, extending losses for a seventh consecutive session.

The stock has lost 3.19% between January 8 and January 15, versus a 0.33% rise in the S&P 500 over the same period.

TTWO has been in the spotlight after a group of staff sacked by its subsidiary Rockstar Games in the U.K. were denied interim pay relief by a tribunal. The employees claimed they were dismissed for attempting to unionize, while Rockstar said the terminations were due to sharing confidential information in a public Discord server.

A Glasgow tribunal ruled it could not grant interim relief, citing uncertainty over whether union membership was the principal reason for the dismissals.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system, TTWO is rated Hold, with a score of 3.33 out of 5. The stock carries an A+ in growth, an A- in revisions, and a B in momentum, partly offset by weaker grades of D in profitability and F in valuation.

Seeking Alpha analysts remain cautious, rating the stock Hold.

Turning to the Wall Street, 27 of 29 analysts rate TTWO buy or higher, one recommends hold, and one suggests strong sell.

An analyst said the stock is “overextended” with a “recent 35% rally and 50x FY27 adjusted EBITDA multiple leave little room for upside, even with Grand Theft Auto VI’s anticipated launch.”

The analyst added that “strong bookings growth and a robust pipeline” are “offset by intensifying competition in casual gaming and a diminishing technical moat.”

Shares have fallen 1.33% over the past month and are down 5.43% year to date.

data.title: Mega-to-large cap healthcare stocks with the strongest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season kicks off, we look at mega-to-large market cap healthcare stocks with strong earnings momentum, which often attract investor attention as positive analyst revisions to earnings estimates can signal improving fundamentals.

The EPS revision rating evaluates how analysts have adjusted their earnings per share estimates for a company over recent periods, with upgrades suggesting growing confidence in a company’s profitability outlook.

The following mega to large cap healthcare sector stocks have earned the highest possible EPS revision rating of A+, indicating the most favorable trajectory of analyst earnings estimate changes.

Here are 10 healthcare stocks that currently hold an A+ EPS revision grade:

  1. Cardinal Health (CAH) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  2. The Ensign Group (ENSG) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  3. HCA Healthcare (HCA) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  4. McKesson (MCK) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  5. Medpace Holdings (MEDP) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  6. Waters Corporation (WAT) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  7. West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  8. Exelixis (EXEL) — EPS Revision Grade: A
  9. Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) — EPS Revision Grade: A
  10. Revvity (RVTY) — EPS Revision Grade: A

Health Care ETFs: (XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), and (FXH)

data.title: Market Voices: Trump, Kremlin on Greenland; Trump on Fed chair nominee
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks.

  • President Trump is threatening to impose tariffs on countries that oppose the U.S. taking over Greenland, which is owned by NATO ally Denmark.

“I may put a tariff on countries if they don’t go along with Greenland, because we need Greenland for national security,” Trump said at a White House event on healthcare on Friday, according to Bloomberg. 

  • The Russian government called the Trump administration's demands regarding Greenland "extraordinary," adding that it was monitoring the situation.

“The situation is unusual; I would even say extraordinary from the standpoint of international law,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov told Russian state news outlet Ria Novosti, according to CNBC.

Trump, Peskov added, “has said that international law is not a priority for him. The situation is developing along a different trajectory, and we, along with the rest of the world, will be watching to see which one.”

  • Trump also said on Friday that he'd prefer to keep Kevin Hassett as director of the National Economic Council rather than nominate him as chair of the Federal Reserve.

“I actually want to keep you where you are, if you want to know the truth," Trump told Hassett during a White House event on Friday, according to CNBC.

Hassett and former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh were believed to be the top two contenders for the Fed chair nomination. Current Fed Chair Jerome Powell's tenure ends in May, although he is slated to remain as a Fed governor through 2028.

data.title: Tractor Supply is cut at Truist due to sales growth concerns
data.description:
Truist Securities downgraded Tractor Supply (TSCO) to a Hold rating after having it slotted at Buy. 

Analyst Scot Ciccarelli said the firm relied on proprietary card data that indicated Tractor Supply (TSCO) sales have continued to broadly fall short of prior expectations. "Further, given 4Q's flattish comp performance (based on our Truist Card Data), we think '26 could be another below-algo year (4th straight below 3%-5%)," he warned.

Looking ahead, a lack of positive catalysts, the potential for incremental rural competition from both Walmart (WMT) and Amazon (AMZN), and concerns about multiple compression are considered solid reasons to stay on the sidelines.

Truist lowered its price target on Tractor Supply (TSCO) to $55 from $67. 

Tractor Supply (TSCO) is scheduled to release its earnings report on January 29. Analysts expect revenue of $15.6B and EPS of $2.11 to be disclosed. Tractor Supply (TSCO) has missed revenue expectations in 11 out of its last 12 quarters.

Shares of Tractor Supply (TSCO) are down 3.3% over the last six weeks.

data.title: Kimberly-Clark acquisition of Kenvue gains ISS recommendation
data.description:
• Institutional Shareholder Services has recommended that shareholders approve Kimberly-Clark's (KMB) proposed ~$49B acquisition of Kenvue (KVUE) that was announced in November.
  • ISS said that the combination would provide important synergies and benefits for Kimberly-Clark's strategic goals, Reuters reported. 
  • Shareholders will vote on the proposed deal on Jan. 29.

data.title: NextEra Energy set for seven straight sessions of gains
data.description:
NextEra Energy (NEE) shares are on track to continue gains for seven straight sessions on Friday, as the stock rose over 2% at $83.94 in afternoon trading.

The company gained over 3% in the preceding six sessions. Overall, the stock gained over 12% last year, compared to the over 16% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

NEE is up over 4% over the past one month. The stock closed marginally high on Thursday at $82.19.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, NEE has a Hold rating with a score of 3.16 out of 5. The company received an A- in the prospect of profitability, while it got a D- in the valuation factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 16 analysts gave NEE a Buy and above, seven analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and one recommended Strong Sell. Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

“The company's recent string of deals with Google and Meta reinforces the argument that it's not investing in capex just for the sake of investing in capex. The balance sheet supports these investments as well. NEE's capex commitments set it up for 8%+ annual adjusted EPS growth for the foreseeable future,” noted a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Earlier in December, the company also raised guidance for adjusted earnings for the current year and next year, as it benefits from a surge in power demand driven by data centers.

data.title: Pnc expects 8% loan growth and 11% revenue increase in 2026 while integrating FirstBank
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The PNC Financial Services Group, Inc. (PNC) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO William Demchak highlighted, "we earned $7 billion in net income or $16.59 per share. Strong performance across all our lines of business resulted in record revenue, 5% operating leverage and 21% EPS growth for the year." He announced the closing of the FirstBank acquisition on January 5 and noted, "we are poised to accelerate that growth in 2026," with expectations for FirstBank to add approximately $1 per share to 2027 results. Demchak emphasized ongoing investments: "one of the largest investment agendas we've ever pursued, including all of our technology initiatives, payments capabilities and consumer rewards platforms and of course, our branch expansions."

  • CFO Robert Reilly reported, "loans of $328 billion grew by $2 billion or 1%" and "deposit balances were up $8 billion or 2% and averaged $440 billion." He stated, "our tangible book value of $112.51 per common share increased 4% linked quarter and 18% compared to the same period a year ago." Reilly added, "we returned $1.1 billion of capital to shareholders," and outlined plans to "increase our quarterly share repurchases to a range of $600 million to $700 million."

Outlook

  • Reilly provided full-year 2026 outlook: "we expect full year average loan growth to be approximately 8%. We expect total revenue to be up approximately 11%. Inside of that, our expectation is for net interest income to be up approximately 14%, and noninterest income to grow 6%. Noninterest expense to be up approximately 7% excluding an estimated $325 million of integration expense." He also projected an effective tax rate of approximately 19.5% and "400 basis points of positive operating leverage, nearly all of which is driven by PNC on a stand-alone basis."

  • For Q1 2026, guidance includes "average loans to be up approximately 5%. Net interest income to be up approximately 6%. Fee income to be down 1% to 2%. Other noninterest income to be in the range of $150 million to $200 million. Taking the component pieces of revenue together, we expect total revenue to be up 2% to 3%."

Financial Results

  • Reilly stated, "total revenue was a record $6.1 billion and grew $156 million or 3%. Noninterest expense of $3.6 billion increased $142 million or 4%. And as a result, we delivered record PPNR of $2.5 billion." Fourth quarter net income was reported as $2 billion or $4.88 per diluted share. Net interest income was $3.7 billion, up $83 million or 2%, and net interest margin was 2.84%. Noninterest income of $2.3 billion increased $73 million or 3%.

  • Asset management and brokerage increased $7 million or 2%. Capital markets and advisory revenue increased $57 million or 13% driven by M&A advisory revenue. Mortgage revenue decreased $13 million or 8%. The Visa derivative adjustment in the fourth quarter was negative $41 million.

  • Credit quality remained strong with nonperforming loans representing 0.67% of total loans at year-end, total delinquencies at 0.44%, and net loan charge-offs at $162 million. Allowance for credit losses was $5.2 billion or 1.58% of total loans.

Q&A

  • John Pancari, Evercore ISI: Asked about the sustainability of increased share repurchases, to which Reilly responded, "$600 million to $700 million is a quarterly pace that we expect to continue through '26."

  • Pancari followed up on CET1 targets; Reilly said, "we've said 10%. So in the first quarter, we'll be in that 10.2%, 10.3%, working our way down from 10.6%."

  • Robert Siefers, Piper Sandler: Inquired about NII momentum; Reilly responded, "guidance with FirstBank for the year...is up 14% in NII. Inside of that...PNC stand-alone, we're somewhere between 7.5% and 8%."

  • Betsy Graseck, Morgan Stanley: Sought details on technology investment. Demchak explained, "we maybe spend $3.5 billion and it's going to go up 10% plus or minus for the year. And inside of that, AI is 20% of that increase."

  • Steven Chubak, Wolfe Research: Asked about NIM trajectory; Reilly stated, "our plans in '26 are to reach that 3% level in the second half of '26, somewhere during the third quarter, maybe the end of the third quarter."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability of capital returns, NII momentum, and the integration of FirstBank, pressing for clarity on guidance and strategic investments. The tone was neutral, with some positive acknowledgment of operating leverage and capital management.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing strong growth, capital flexibility, and investment discipline. Responses were direct and assertive, as seen in Demchak’s and Reilly’s explanations regarding capital strategy and technology investment priorities.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained confident, but with increased emphasis on integration of FirstBank and future growth targets. Analyst tone was similarly persistent, with recurring questions on growth sustainability and capital allocation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from stable-to-modest growth in Q3 to more robust targets in Q4, specifically with full-year 2026 expectations for 8% loan growth and 11% revenue growth, largely attributed to the FirstBank acquisition and organic expansion.

  • Strategic focus expanded from organic growth and ongoing branch builds to include a major acquisition and aggressive investment in technology and automation.

  • Analyst questions in both quarters centered on NII, capital returns, and deposit dynamics, but Q4 featured more follow-up on integration of FirstBank and technology spend.

  • Key metrics saw higher loan and deposit balances, record revenue, and improved tangible book value in Q4. Management’s confidence increased, highlighting large-scale investment and integration efforts.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited integration risks with FirstBank, noting "nonrecurring merger and integration costs is approximately $325 million, the majority of which will be recognized in the first half of 2026."

  • Reilly stated, "CRE balances have largely stabilized, and we anticipate moderate growth in 2026," but acknowledged potential headwinds in residential real estate loans.

  • Economic risks were mainly characterized as external/exogenous, with Demchak saying, "It has to be some exogenous variable because the base economy, I just don't see big cracks that are going to be realized in '26."

Final Takeaway

PNC capped a strong year with record financial results and the transformational acquisition of FirstBank, setting the stage for accelerated growth in 2026. Management projects significant increases in loans, revenues, and operating leverage, underpinned by disciplined capital returns and continued investments in technology and branch expansion. The company is committed to integrating FirstBank efficiently while targeting substantial operating and revenue synergies, supporting strong forward momentum into the coming year.
